scheme_name: All the candidates must fulfill the eligibility criteria for Mukhyamantri Kanyadan Yojana
description: Applicant must be a permanent resident of the Jharkhand state. The annual income of the family of the applicant from all sources must not exceed Rs. 72000. The bride and bridegroom must be of minimum legal age of the marriage. The age of the girl must be above 18 years and the age of boy must be more than 21 years. All the applicants must belong to the Below Poverty Line (BPL) category. No member of the family of the applicant must be employed in any state or central govt. services. The financial assistance is not applicable in the case of Re-marriage (पुर्नविवाह). However, widows can avail the scheme benefits in case of Re-marriage.
full_text:
All the candidates must fulfill the eligibility criteria for Mukhyamantri Kanyadan Yojana
Applicant must be a permanent resident of the Jharkhand state.
The annual income of the family of the applicant from all sources must not exceed Rs. 72000.
The bride and bridegroom must be of minimum legal age of the marriage. The age of the girl must be above 18 years and the age of boy must be more than 21 years.
All the applicants must belong to the Below Poverty Line (BPL) category.
No member of the family of the applicant must be employed in any state or central govt. services.
The financial assistance is not applicable in the case of Re-marriage (पुर्नविवाह). However, widows can avail the scheme benefits in case of Re-marriage.

Orphan girls who does not belong to BPL category are also eligible and can avail assistance under Mukhyamantri Kanyadan Yojana.

scheme_name: Bal Garib Samridhhi Yojana
description: Anyone would be able to contribute to the scheme. The details of the scheme has not been revealed yet because the scheme is not launched yet. The state government is working to build child reform homes and rehabilitation centers in Ranchi and Gumla where they would provide skill development training which would help to check migration among children. This would cost around Rs. 23 crore and will be borne by the government itself. Model child reform homes has been constructed in Ranchi and Jamshedpur and the work is under progress at other child reform centers. Through a video conferencing, around 115 cases of juvenile justice were taken up of which around 17 cases were disposed off. Video conferencing facility would be provided at Hazaribagh child reform centers.
full_text:
Bal Garib Samridhhi Yojana
Anyone would be able to contribute to the scheme. The details of the scheme has not been revealed yet because the scheme is not launched yet. The state government is working to build child reform homes and rehabilitation centers in Ranchi and Gumla where they would provide skill development training which would help to check migration among children. This would cost around Rs. 23 crore and will be borne by the government itself.
Model child reform homes has been constructed in Ranchi and Jamshedpur and the work is under progress at other child reform centers. Through a video conferencing, around 115 cases of juvenile justice were taken up of which around 17 cases were disposed off. Video conferencing facility would be provided at Hazaribagh child reform centers.

Panchayat Secretariat would conduct a survey regarding the number of orphan children in the area and these orphan children would later be admitted at these rehabilitation centers where they would be provided training and education for better future.
Besides the announcement of Bal Garib Samridhhi Yojana, the government has also announced that new welfare schemes for betterment and upliftment of villages, poor, farmers and tribal will also be launched soon.

full_text:
Benefits Under Mukhyamantri Swasthya Beema Yojana (MSBY)
The state government would provide health insurance coverage to the state residents, especially families belonging to the economically weaker section of the society under the MSBY scheme.
Under the scheme, Rs. 50,000 has been provisioned for secondary care and Rs. 2 lakh for special care, Rs. 30,000 for senior citizens of additionally covered class. The government would provide health cards to the beneficiaries and the whole system would be cashless.

The MSBY scheme, once implemented would be of great help to the state residents especially those who are not able to afford today’s costly treatments.

scheme_name: Financial Assistance Under PMAY-G
description: The central government would be providing a financial assistance of Rs. Rs. 120,000 in plain areas and Rs. 130,000 in eastern, hilly and IAP areas to the beneficiaries. Along with this, an additional Rs. 12000 will also be provided to build toilet to each beneficiary under the Swachch Bharat Mission. The beneficiaries will also be provided daily wages of up to 90-95 days (Rs. 18000) through MGNREGA. Hence, the total financial assistance amount will be 1 Lakh 50 thousand for plain areas and 1 Lakh 60 thousand for eastern, hilly and IAP areas. Interested beneficiaries can also avail a loan of upto Rs. 70000 from any of the nationalized banks for the construction of home.
full_text:
Financial Assistance Under PMAY-G
The central government would be providing a financial assistance of Rs. Rs. 120,000 in plain areas and Rs. 130,000 in eastern, hilly and IAP areas to the beneficiaries. Along with this, an additional Rs. 12000 will also be provided to build toilet to each beneficiary under the Swachch Bharat Mission. The beneficiaries will also be provided daily wages of up to 90-95 days (Rs. 18000) through MGNREGA.
Hence, the total financial assistance amount will be 1 Lakh 50 thousand for plain areas and 1 Lakh 60 thousand for eastern, hilly and IAP areas. Interested beneficiaries can also avail a loan of upto Rs. 70000 from any of the nationalized banks for the construction of home.
Selection of beneficiaries under the Pradhan Mantri Awas Yojana – Gramin will be done on the basis of SECC-2011 data. The government has also launched AwaasApp for beneficiaries to know the status of their application, funding or share the progress of construction.

scheme_name: Gyanodaya Scheme – Free Tablet to Teachers
description: The aim of this scheme is not only the real time monitoring of schools but it will also help in knowing online the learning outcomes of children. Through online software, teachers, children’s attendance, drop out, mid- day meal etc. will be monitored online by the department of education, Jharkhand.
full_text:
Gyanodaya Scheme – Free Tablet to Teachers
The aim of this scheme is not only the real time monitoring of schools but it will also help in knowing online the learning outcomes of children. Through online software, teachers, children’s attendance, drop out, mid- day meal etc. will be monitored online by the department of education, Jharkhand.

The NITI aayog has decided to develop the School Education Quality Index to measure the results of the schools across the country. This scheme will also help to measure the performance of school management. Many schemes are also being included to check the activity of schools like e-Vidyavahini, e-mid-day meals and teachers’ English training programs etc.
Recently, the cabinet has given its approval for the Gyanodaya Scheme with the cost of Rs. 63 crores and 60 Lakhs. These scheme will empower the teachers to become a good learner. Through the tablet, the teachers will keep learning new things all the time and acquiring knowledge from different online sources.

Source: http://www.jagran.com/jharkhand/ranchi-teachers-day-celebration-in-jharkhand-16662640.html

description: In order to encourage Higher Education in Jharkhand, the state govt. has launched online Chief Minister Fellowship Portal. Accordingly, Department of Higher and Technical Education will provide fellowship under 3 sections:-
full_text:
Jharkhand CM Fellowship Yojna
In order to encourage Higher Education in Jharkhand, the state govt. has launched online Chief Minister Fellowship Portal. Accordingly, Department of Higher and Technical Education will provide fellowship under 3 sections:-

Graduation level – All the candidates pursuing graduation / technical education in 163 best institutions which includes – 23 IIT, 19 IIM, 29 NIT, 17 Law Universities.
Ph.D Level – All candidates who have completed their PG Course and are pursuing Ph.D will get Rs. 15000 per month for the upcoming 3 years for research purpose.
Research Work – If the research subject is accepted by top 100 universities than students will get Rs. 1 lakh.

However, only the students who are not paying IT returns can apply for Chief Minister Fellowship Yojana.

scheme_name: Jharkhand Divyang Jan Adhikar Niyamavali – 2018
description: The important features of Divyang Jan Rights Rules 2018 are as follows:-
full_text:
Jharkhand Divyang Jan Adhikar Niyamavali – 2018
The important features of Divyang Jan Rights Rules 2018 are as follows:-

Under this new scheme, all the persons with disabilities will get equal opportunities both in government and private jobs. Accordingly to achieve this target, each office establishment shall maintain the records of the divyang employees. Moreover, every department must show the number of appointments and vacancies existing for disabled.
Jharkhand govt. is also going to set up a state level commission to resolve the complaints filed by the PWDs. State disability commissioner can take strict action on those who are found guilty of discrimination to these disabled persons.
The state govt. will also revise the stipends for such people. Even a new special committee is to be formed to identify and locate disabled persons across the entire state.
National institute of Locomotor Disabilities shall run a composite regional center for disabled people in the state. This CRC will focus on their rehabilitation and also on their special education.

Cabinet committee also allotted a 3 acre plot at Dubalia in Kanke block to Kolkata-based Rashtriya Gatishil Divyang Sangathan to set up CRC.

scheme_name: Jharkhand Free LPG Cylinders Refill to PMUY Beneficiaries
description: All the beneficiaries of the flagship Pradhan Mantri Ujjwala Yojana (PMUY) scheme in Jharkhand can now avail a free refill of their LPG cylinders. The state govt. of Jharkhand claims that this is a first-of-its-kind initiative in the country. Jharkhand govt. will start process of refill of cylinders along with distribution of 12 lakh new LPG gas connections. The process of Jhakhand Free LPG Cylinders Refill to PMUY beneficiaries will start from 23 August 2019 across the state. Jharkhand is the only state in India where beneficiaries of PMUY scheme are given free ovens and filled cylinders during distribution of LPG gas connections. Now, people can refill their cylinders for free.
full_text:
Jharkhand Free LPG Cylinders Refill to PMUY Beneficiaries
All the beneficiaries of the flagship Pradhan Mantri Ujjwala Yojana (PMUY) scheme in Jharkhand can now avail a free refill of their LPG cylinders. The state govt. of Jharkhand claims that this is a first-of-its-kind initiative in the country. Jharkhand govt. will start process of refill of cylinders along with distribution of 12 lakh new LPG gas connections.
The process of Jhakhand Free LPG Cylinders Refill to PMUY beneficiaries will start from 23 August 2019 across the state. Jharkhand is the only state in India where beneficiaries of PMUY scheme are given free ovens and filled cylinders during distribution of LPG gas connections. Now, people can refill their cylinders for free.

Since the launch of PMUY scheme in Jharkhand in 2016, around 31 lakh free LPG gas connections have already been distributed in the Jharkhand state.
The PMUY scheme aims to replace the hazardous cooking fuels used in rural India with clean and efficient LPG fuel. All the families living below poverty line (BPL) are the beneficiaries of PM Ujjwala Yojana. For more details, visit the official website at https://pmuy.gov.in/

Content Source / Reference Link: https://www.moneycontrol.com/news/economy/policy/jharkhand-pmuy-beneficiaries-to-get-free-lpg-cylinders-refill-4286551.html

scheme_name: Jharkhand Free Smartphone Scheme for 28 Lakh Farmers
description: The state govt. of Jharkhand will launch a Free Smartphone Scheme to provide free mobile phones to around 28 lakh farmers. The announcement of this Free mobile Scheme in Jharkhand will take place in the next budget i.e 2019-20. Farmers will be able to connect to e-NAM which is an online trading platform for agriculture commodities in India. e-NAM market facilitates farmers, traders and buyers with online trading in commodities. This market is also helpful in better price discovery and provide facilities for smooth marketing of their produce. The state govt. has recently sent several farmers to Israel so as to enable them to learn to maximize their farming activities with limited resources.
full_text:
Jharkhand Free Smartphone Scheme for 28 Lakh Farmers
The state govt. of Jharkhand will launch a Free Smartphone Scheme to provide free mobile phones to around 28 lakh farmers. The announcement of this Free mobile Scheme in Jharkhand will take place in the next budget i.e 2019-20. Farmers will be able to connect to e-NAM which is an online trading platform for agriculture commodities in India.
e-NAM market facilitates farmers, traders and buyers with online trading in commodities. This market is also helpful in better price discovery and provide facilities for smooth marketing of their produce. The state govt. has recently sent several farmers to Israel so as to enable them to learn to maximize their farming activities with limited resources.

Agriculture sector is the largest contributor to the economy and the gross domestic product (GDP) of the country. This sector involves more than 50% of the total population in India and generates direct and indirect employment which is essential for the growth of the economy.

full_text:
Jharkhand Mukhya Mantri Sukanya Yojana Beneficiary Selection
Around 36,57,323 families would get benefited from Jharkhand Mukhyamantri Sukanya Yojana 2019. As per the SECC 2011 data, there are 27,46,106 eligible beneficiaries for the scheme in Jharkhand. Moreover around 9,11,271 Antyodaya Ration Card holders (as on 21 Dec 2018) would also get included in this Jharkhand Mukhya Mantri Sukanya Yojana.
In 5 districts namely Deoghar, Godda, Koderma, Giridih and Palamu, a large number of cases for child marriage has been emerged. So there is a need to provide special attention to those regions in the state of Jharkhand.

पहले पढ़ाई फिर विदाई। इसी मूलमंत्र के साथ हमारी सरकार मुख्यमंत्री सुकन्या योजना लेकर आई है। बेटियों को खूब पढ़ाइए, हर कदम पर सरकार आपके साथ है।#NewJharkhand pic.twitter.com/wHAe4uKkCu
— Raghubar Das (@dasraghubar) January 4, 2019

At the time of nomination for class 1st, 6th, 9th and 11th, the financial amount would be given through DBT directly into the bank accounts of girls. This scheme will reduce the drop out rate of the girl child, curb child marriage and would also reduce corruption.
By providing the girls with financial aid, the govt. will ensure the better upbringing of girls. Financial incentives would be given to those who completed their 18 years of age without marriage. This is an important step to curb child marriage which will lead to the social upliftment of women.

scheme_name: Jharkhand Mukhya Mantri Sukanya Yojana in Hindi
description: राज्य योजना अंतर्गत “मुख्यमंत्री सुकन्या योजना” की स्वीकृति दी गई. झारखंड के 36 लाख 57 हजार 323 परिवार को इसका लाभ मिलेगा। इस योजना का मुख्य उद्देश्य महिला सशक्तिकरण को बढ़ावा देना, बालिका शिक्षा को प्रोत्साहित करना एवं बाल विवाह प्रथा का अंत करना है. इस योजना के तहत SECC-2011 (ग्रामीण) के अनुसार राज्य के योग्य लाभुक परिवार जिनकी संख्या 27,46,106 है एवं राज्य के सभी अंत्योदय राशन कार्डधारी परिवार जिनकी संख्या दिनांक 21 दिसम्बर, 2018 को 9,11,217 है, को मुख्यमंत्री सुकन्या योजना अंतर्गत बालिकाओं के कल्याण हेतु निम्न प्रकार आर्थिक सहायता प्रदान की जाएगी. इस प्रकार झारखंड के 36 लाख 57 हजार 323 परिवार को इसका लाभ मिलेगा।
full_text:
Jharkhand Mukhya Mantri Sukanya Yojana in Hindi
राज्य योजना अंतर्गत “मुख्यमंत्री सुकन्या योजना” की स्वीकृति दी गई. झारखंड के 36 लाख 57 हजार 323 परिवार को इसका लाभ मिलेगा। इस योजना का मुख्य उद्देश्य महिला सशक्तिकरण को बढ़ावा देना, बालिका शिक्षा को प्रोत्साहित करना एवं बाल विवाह प्रथा का अंत करना है. इस योजना के तहत SECC-2011 (ग्रामीण) के अनुसार राज्य के योग्य लाभुक परिवार जिनकी संख्या 27,46,106 है एवं राज्य के सभी अंत्योदय राशन कार्डधारी परिवार जिनकी संख्या दिनांक 21 दिसम्बर, 2018 को 9,11,217 है, को मुख्यमंत्री सुकन्या योजना अंतर्गत बालिकाओं के कल्याण हेतु निम्न प्रकार आर्थिक सहायता प्रदान की जाएगी. इस प्रकार झारखंड के 36 लाख 57 हजार 323 परिवार को इसका लाभ मिलेगा।
मुख्यमंत्री सुकन्या योजना के तहत जन्म से 2 वर्ष उम्र तक की बालिका को आर्थिक लाभ ₹5000 दी जाएगी. कक्षा 1 में नामांकित होने वाली बालिका को ₹5000 की आर्थिक सहायता राशि दी जाएगी. पांचवी कक्षा उत्तीर्ण होने के पश्चात ₹5000 की सहायता राशि दी जाएगी. इसी प्रकार कक्षा आठवीं, कक्षा 10वीं एवं कक्षा बारहवीं उत्तीर्ण होने के पश्चात ₹5000 की आर्थिक सहायता राशि का लाभ मिलेगा.18 से 20 वर्ष उम्र की व्यस्क और अविवाहित को ₹10000 की आर्थिक सहायता राशि दी जाएगी.

झारखण्ड में आज से मुख्यमंत्री सुकन्या योजना की शुरुआत हो रही है। अब हमारी बेटियों के लालन-पालन और शिक्षा में नहीं आएगी कोई बाधा, ये है हमारा आपसे वादा। पहले पढ़ाई फिर विदाई का मूल मंत्र अपनाएं, बेटियों को आगे बढ़ाएं।#NewJharkhand pic.twitter.com/Pv8GS1aYcS
— Raghubar Das (@dasraghubar) January 24, 2019

full_text:
Jharkhand Mukhyamantri Annapurna Yojana
The state govt. will provide meals to poor hungry people in just Rs. 10 under Mukhyamantri Annapoorna Yojana. full_text:
Jharkhand Mukhyamantri Krishi Ashirwad Yojana 2019
Under Mukhyamantri Krishi Aashirwad Yojana, govt. will provide Rs. 5,000 per acre per annum to farmers for Kharif crops. All the farmers whose land holdings are less than 1 acre would also get minimum Rs. 5,000 every year. This assistance amount would be given through cheques under Jharkhand Mukhyamantri Krishi Ashirwad Yojana 2019. Around 22.76 lakh small and marginal farmers would be benefited through this scheme.
Govt. will make a budgetary provision of Rs. 2250 crore for this Jharkhand Mukhyamantri Krishi Aashirwad Yojana 2019-20. Around 45 lakh acres agricultural land would be covered under this Krishi Ashirwad Scheme. Now the farmers will not have to depend on any banks or money lenders to buy seeds (beej), compost (khaad) and to make other agricultural investment.

Jaharkhand Mukhyamantri Krishi Ashirwad Yojana 2019 is an investment support scheme which will prevent farmers from taking loans as it will make them self dependent. The state govt. is consistently working to improve the financial conditions of farmers. Govt. is making loans available for farmers @0% interest rate. Moreover, govt. is paying Rs. 66 crore as premium for fasal bima (crop insurance) of 14.85 lakh farmers.

full_text:
Jharkhand Old Age / Widow / Viklang Pension to Raise
In the Jharhand Budget 2019-20, the state govt. will make an announcement regarding the increase in the Old Age Pension, Widow (Vidhwa) Pension and Viklang Pension scheme. Now, all of these social security pension beneficiaries will get an increased amount from Rs. 600 to Rs. 1000 from next year.

Moreover, the directly credit going into the beneficiary account would also help in eliminating the need of middlemen and thus reducing middlemen. The state govt. is consistently working towards ensuring that the pension scheme benefits reaches the last person of the society.

scheme_name: Jharkhand Paddy Procurement Scheme to Purchase Paddy from Farmers
description: The state govt. of Jharkhand has fixed a target to procure 4 lakh metric tonne of paddy from farmers. The Paddy Procurement Scheme (Dhaan Adhiprapti Yojana) will start from 1 December 2018 and will end on 31 March 2019. Till date, there are 239 paddy procurement centers in the state and the numbers of these centers can be increased by the concerned department if needed. Farmers will get Rs. 1750 for one quintal of paddy as per the MSP fixed by the Modi government. The payment of paddy will go directly into the bank accounts of the farmers through NEFT / RTGS or DBT modes. Bonus is being under consideration and the concerned department is going to submit the report on expenses incurred for bonus. After consideration of this report, the final decision on bonus would be taken.
full_text:
Jharkhand Paddy Procurement Scheme to Purchase Paddy from Farmers
The state govt. of Jharkhand has fixed a target to procure 4 lakh metric tonne of paddy from farmers. The Paddy Procurement Scheme (Dhaan Adhiprapti Yojana) will start from 1 December 2018 and will end on 31 March 2019. Till date, there are 239 paddy procurement centers in the state and the numbers of these centers can be increased by the concerned department if needed.
Farmers will get Rs. 1750 for one quintal of paddy as per the MSP fixed by the Modi government. The payment of paddy will go directly into the bank accounts of the farmers through NEFT / RTGS or DBT modes. Bonus is being under consideration and the concerned department is going to submit the report on expenses incurred for bonus. After consideration of this report, the final decision on bonus would be taken.

All the farmers would get information regarding selling of their paddy through SMS or mobile phones. Farmers can then contact the paddy procurement centers to get the token and date to sell their dhaan. Moreover, all those farmers who have not obtained token will again get SMS on their mobile phones to sell their crops in a sequence wise manner. Under Santhal Pargana division, the state govt. will procure paddy through decentralized system in districts Godda, Deoghar, Dumka, Jamtara, Sahibganj and Pakur.

एक दिसंबर से न्यूनतम समर्थन मूल्य पर राज्य सरकार 239 केंद्रों पर धान खरीदेगी। अन्नदाता किसानों को प्रति क्विंटल 1750 रुपये समर्थन मूल्य का भुगतान किया जाएगा। #AgriSummit2018 pic.twitter.com/ByBpqQE9XP
— Raghubar Das (@dasraghubar) November 28, 2018

In Palamu division (Palamu, Garhwa, Latehar), the state govt. has choosen the FCI as the nodal agency for procurement. In North Chotanagpur division (Chatra, Hazaribagh, Koderma, Giridih, Ramgarh, Bokaro, Dhanbad), South Chotanagpur division (Lohardaga, Gumla, Simdega, Ranchi, Khunti), Kolhan division (West Singhbhum, Saraikela Kharsawan, East Singhbhum) and Santhal Pargana division (Godda, Deoghar, Dumka, Jamtara, Sahibganj and Pakur), procurement would be done through Jharkhand State Food and Civil Supplies Corporation Ltd.
The state govt. has allocated a fund of Rs. 200 crore for paddy procurement (dhaan adhiprapti) which was previously reserved for sugar procurement. All the farmers will get prices of their paddy within 15 days after selling their crops at the procurement centers.

scheme_name: Jharkhand Sahiya Arogya Kunji Yojana 2019
description: CM has started this Sahya Arogya Kunji Scheme on 19 January and as a official mark, had given arogya kunji (medical kits) to 5 shiya each of Chatra and Latehar district. Mukhyamantri had reached at a function to start arogya kunji, bike ambulance and medical cube scheme. This is a new scheme which would enable people to avail better health services in villages. Now people would not have to run to the cities to get better treatment. With the help of these medical kits or aarogya kunji, sahiya would be able to provide better healthcare facilities to rural people. Previously, the state gov. has also launched the flagship health care scheme launched by PM narendra Modi namely, Ayushman Bharat – PM Jan Arogya Yojana. AB-PMJAY is the world’s largest scheme which provides free and cashless treatment upto Rs. 5 lakh to poor people.
full_text:
Jharkhand Sahiya Arogya Kunji Yojana 2019
CM has started this Sahya Arogya Kunji Scheme on 19 January and as a official mark, had given arogya kunji (medical kits) to 5 shiya each of Chatra and Latehar district. Mukhyamantri had reached at a function to start arogya kunji, bike ambulance and medical cube scheme. This is a new scheme which would enable people to avail better health services in villages. Now people would not have to run to the cities to get better treatment.
With the help of these medical kits or aarogya kunji, sahiya would be able to provide better healthcare facilities to rural people. Previously, the state gov. has also launched the flagship health care scheme launched by PM narendra Modi namely, Ayushman Bharat – PM Jan Arogya Yojana. AB-PMJAY is the world’s largest scheme which provides free and cashless treatment upto Rs. 5 lakh to poor people.

For Ayushman Bharat Yojana in Jharkhand, the state govt. has made a budgetary provision of Rs. 400 crore. Around 57 lakh families (out of total 68 lakh families) of the state are going to be benefited from this National Health Protection Scheme. The complete premium amount for this PM Jan Arogya Yojana in Jharkhand would be borne by the government.

सहिया आरोग्य कुंजी के माध्यम से सुदूर इलाकों में भी स्वास्थ्य सेवाएं उपलब्ध होंगी। हमारी सरकार 22 जनवरी को बजट में सहिया आरोग्य कुंजी योजना का भी प्रावधान करेगी। यह योजना पूरे राज्य में लागू की जाएगी। #NewJharkhand pic.twitter.com/cIChACun3X
— Raghubar Das (@dasraghubar) January 19, 2019

PM Rashtriya Swasthya Suraksha Mission or Ayushman Bharat Scheme covers all those people whose name appears in the SECC 2011 database. The state govt. of Jharkhand is working on the mission of ensuring Health For All with an objective to create New India by FY 2022.

scheme_name: Jharkhand SC / ST Civil Seva Protsahan Yojana
description: The scheme to encourage students from SC /ST category has been launched on 5th November 2018 in a cabinet meeting chaired by CM Raghubar Das. Now, all the SC / ST students who have cleared UPSC civil services preliminary (PT) exam will get an assistance of Rs. 1 lakh to prepare for the civil services mains examination under SC / ST Civil Seva Protsahan Yojana in Jharkhand. Only such SC / ST students who have passed their intermediate (class 12th) and graduation from the state. This assistance can only be availed for a single time and the annual family income of the students from all sources must not exceed Rs. 2.5 lakh.
full_text:
Jharkhand SC / ST Civil Seva Protsahan Yojana
The scheme to encourage students from SC /ST category has been launched on 5th November 2018 in a cabinet meeting chaired by CM Raghubar Das. Now, all the SC / ST students who have cleared UPSC civil services preliminary (PT) exam will get an assistance of Rs. 1 lakh to prepare for the civil services mains examination under SC / ST Civil Seva Protsahan Yojana in Jharkhand.
Only such SC / ST students who have passed their intermediate (class 12th) and graduation from the state. This assistance can only be availed for a single time and the annual family income of the students from all sources must not exceed Rs. 2.5 lakh.

Even those candidates who are already taking benefits of coaching from either the state govt. or central govt. are also not eligible.

SC/ST छात्र-छात्राओं को संघ लोक सेवा आयोग द्वारा आयोजित सिविल सेवा परीक्षा की प्रारंभिक परीक्षा में उत्तीर्ण होने पर मुख्य परीक्षा / साक्षात्कार की तैयारी हेतु एक लाख रुपये की आर्थिक सहायता प्रदान की जायेगी। #NewJharkhand
— Raghubar Das (@dasraghubar) November 5, 2018

SC/ST students will not have to face financial constraints for further studies after passing UPSC prelims. #NewJharkhand pic.twitter.com/m5KP4wR46F
— Raghubar Das (@dasraghubar) November 6, 2018

Generally students have to move away from their homes to avail better study facilities in order to carry on their preparation for the civil services examination. So, all the bright candidates who have already cracked PT (preliminary test) of UPSC civil services exam will not have to think about expenses rather they must focus on the preparation for their Mains examination.

The door to door distribution of rice packets will be handled by the employees of Food and Civil Supplies Department. The government has formally launched the free rice scheme in three districts including, Godda, Sahebganj and Palamu. full_text:
PTG Dakia Yojana – Free Rice Scheme
Under the PTG Dakia Yojana, a free rice scheme under which Primitive Tribal Group families will be provided a packet of 35 kg rice every month. The door to door distribution of rice packets will be handled by the employees of Food and Civil Supplies Department.
The government has formally launched the free rice scheme in three districts including, Godda, Sahebganj and Palamu. The above areas are selected for the launch of the scheme because they have a relatively higher concentration of PTGs. On an average, every district in Jharkhand have about 5,000 PGT families but in these districts, the average number of PGT families is about 7,000.

Within the month, the scheme will be started for benefiting PTG families in all districts of the state. Women self-help groups will manage the work of creating rice packets which would help to make them financially strong.
Total 32 tribal groups are living in Jharkhand state of which only eight group have been categorized as PGTs including, Asur, Birhor, Paharia (Baiga), Sabar, Birajiya, Korwa, Mal Pahariya and Souriya Paharia.

As per the information, these selected PGTs are low on literacy and the average monthly income of these families is Rs 1,000 or even less. Usually, these families remain aloof from the usual settlements. Under the scheme, the government would give them only rice, and not wheat, as that is what the PTGs prefer in their diet.
